title: Protecting our FLOSS commons from LLMs — Codeberg News
content_html:
In Brief:

  • Two motions regarding "artificial intelligence" and Large Language Models (LLMs) were voted on among Codeberg e. V. members and passed.
  • We are promising to not use any of your data to train LLM and explain what the planned Terms of Use change mean for 'vibe-coded' projects.
  • We believe that LLMs endanger the free/libre software ecosystem as a whole.

The Codeberg e. V. annual assembly is the meeting that puts power into the hand of our active members. Proposals are discussed live, and later voted on asynchronously.

Since Large Language Models (LLMs) are an emerging but controversial technology, it is not surprising that two of the votes were concerned with Codeberg's position about this technology. The 14-day voting period ended yesterday and both proposals were accepted.

The first vote was a statement about Codeberg e. V.'s stance on using your data to train LLMs.

As stated in our privacy policy, "We do not want to need your data", and this also holds for the use of our user and project data for using or training generative "AI": The Codeberg forge and its associated services are not and will not use the code or data of projects and users to train "Artificial Intelligence" tools such as Large Language Models, whose purpose is to create output modelled after their training input. As an association, we believe that these technologies are incompatible with responsibly creating and maintaining free & open source software.

The second vote was more controversial, but was also accepted with 358 agreements vs 144 disagreements (and 14 abstentions), with a high voter turn-out of around 50% of active members. It implies a change to our terms of use to prohibit 'vibe-coded projects'. We'll share thoughts about the practical impact at the end of the article.

We all pay for hungry LLMs

LLMs are a very costly technology, and those costs keep rising as the companies providing them have to start recouping their investments. They are not only costly for those who use and explicitly subscribe to these services. The costs are not only hidden in 'normal' cloud and service subscriptions that cross-finance the 'innovative new features' you never asked for. LLMs are so costly that companies externalize the costs on a massive scale - on those who don't use them and society at large. Increased hardware prices, energy use and environmental damage - we all pay for it!

Strained servers due to nonsensical crawling

In past posts we have already outlined how our infrastructure at Codeberg is regularly put under heavy load from webcrawlers of those companies who plan to ingest all of the code that is hosted on Codeberg for training their LLMs.

At Codeberg, we are happy to provide free and open access to code. Just run git clone and enjoy.

Unfortunately, these crawlers instead try to read every single page from Codeberg, no matter if it makes sense. This includes all the different issue filter variants, Git history, as well as the actual files at any point in Git history - even if they are still equal.

These needless accesses create expensive database queries that diminish the service quality for all of us, requires substantial amounts of work from our system administrators, and force us to spend time building defensive mechanisms instead of cool new stuff. Mechanisms that also affect new and existing legitimate users, as we're having to impose limits or outright blocks on their desired workflow; leaving them a worse experience with Codeberg.

The development team of none

Using LLMs to work with your code gives you a kick of adrenaline. You can develop at a rapid pace, build things as if you had a large team. Only that you have none. In fact, you are (often) alone, working with a statistical machine that turns energy into code.

It seems like many ‘vibe coders’ don't realize that they don't actually have a community around them. They build projects as if they had, and spend resources accordingly. We see projects having a lot of code activity, heavy CI/CD testing, frequent and large release binaries. Sometimes, it feels like the amount of supported platforms exceeds the amount of actual users.

To us, it seems ridiculous to see projects with a single developer and virtually no users consuming as much or even more resources than some of the largest community projects on Codeberg, which operate frugal with CI/CD and storage resources. We do not believe it is reasonable for Codeberg to invest our precious donation money into hosting of large ghost projects.

Hardware sourcing is becoming an headache

The training and deployment of LLMs has drastically raised the cost of buying hardware, in particular for SSDs and memory. To give you an example: The type of drive we sourced for € 700 only some years ago has risen to € 3.700 now - and is often out of stock. As a consequence, hosting code on Codeberg is becoming more expensive.

While we are owning our hardware, and are thus not directly impacted by inflating 'cloud' rental costs, it means that replacing or expanding our hardware is now substantially more costly than it used (and needs) to be. While we might be able to afford paying those inflated hardware prices, it is money that we can not spend elsewhere to improve our service and foster the mission of Codeberg.

A growing digital divide

These price hikes also lead to a growing digital divide: Small and even large operators are endangered by rising costs, while only the largest cloud companies have reliable agreements for hardware. Increasing costs for services like website hosting, storage or compute can be challenging to a lot of small NGOs, local coops, research projects and other usage of digital tools that we considered for granted until recently.

Not only does it become more expensive to run digital infrastructure, even more basic digital tools like computers and smartphones are affected by the rising costs, turning personal computing back into a luxury — but now in a world where digital tools are a de-facto requirement to participate in society. Devices with little compute and storage capacity take away sovereignity from users and move them into the cost trap of cloud providers selling those back to you.

Civic infrastructure, its users, and the environment suffer

The negative impacts on infrastructures don't stop there, but also concern more basic civic infrastructure: Due to the energy and water demands that are inherent to the data centers built specifically for training LLMs, many communities already today experience rising consumer costs for both electricity and drinking water. And beyond the rising costs, those living close to the data centers are directly affected by increasing air and noise pollution.

To be able to power up those data centers, the companies behind them are also actively lobbying to be exempt from environmental regulations: In Frankfurt, data centers already now consume 40% of the local electricity, and demand is rising. To meet this demand, they want to use fossil fuels.

Collaboration at danger

It is not purely the digital and civic infrastructures that are impacted by the use of LLMs. The free/libre software ecosystem, of which we consider Codeberg an important part of, is a social phenomenon centered on collaboration. Working in this way is only possible thanks to free sharing and mutual learning. This includes even very small tools that are shared and re-used and around which collaboration can start out. In contrast, by adopting LLMs people tend to code single-use software from scratch. While this leads to an increase in 'shared' code, it is mostly code that not only has not been 'written' by anyone but is also not maintained by anyone.

Losing trust in each other

The widespread use of LLMs in FLOSS is instead becoming a multidimensional attack on the trust between contributors and the very idea of convivial collaboration itself. Maintainers are under an increased work-load due to people submitting (often well-meaning) low-effort, LLM-generated contributions that require substantial amounts of time to review. At the same time it is becoming increasingly less clear which projects are maintained by experienced developers and which ones are LLM-generated without any meaningful human oversight and input. In the case of copyleft projects, LLMs additionally also lead to 'license laundering', where copyleft code is stripped of its reciprocity requirements by 'generating' it out of the training data.

We observe an increasing trend of mistrusting each other, up to the point where people who put in actual effort to analyze issues or share their suggestions are being accused of having used LLMs when they did not. At the same time, others instruct their LLMs to hide their traces and actively avoid common patterns, prompting others in reviewing contributions and communication more carefully for signs of machine generation.

Entering a vicious cycle

Together, these forces make collaboration not only harder but also less rewarding: With the transaction cost of collaboration increasing, people are becoming less likely to contribute to creating high-quality software projects and more likely to 'vibecode' a one-off software that is specific to your need, and won't evolve beyond. We get a vicious cycle where collaboration is becoming less and less rewarding, while the amount of single-use software that's unmaintained and never sees any improvements is going up.

Although often well intentioned, sharing the result of an prompt and calling it "libre software" does not make the world a better place. Codeberg is not and does not want to be a place to dump such generated single-use software that no one else will ever look at. We are a place for people to collaborate and improve software together. Within this context, the recent votes can be understood as a reconfirmation of those principles: As we want to center on human collaboration, we will not actively support or engage in the creation of LLMs and will not put our limited resources to use for storing single-use software that would pollute our FLOSS commons.

Evolving our terms

Changing our Terms of Use sends a strong signal about our mission and projects we want to support. Having said that, you won't see a mass-deletion of content within days. Our moderation team will not start off generating an exhaustive list of affected repositories to remove. Instead, using cases like the examples below we will start operationalizing the new rules. We're humans at the other end, who care deeply about free/libre software projects and communities.

We acknowledge that many developers have started to embrace LLMs as a tool in their workflows. Some use it extensively and rarely code by hand, others delegate only specific tasks to it. We understand that you want to know how the change affects your projects going forward. While we can't give an easy answer, we'll share some remarks that should address most of the concerns raised in the discussion.

Some early, but informal guidelines

If your work fits into these cases, it is unlikely that you are affected at all:

  • Projects who have an active community that cares about and maintains the software
  • Projects with a significant pre-LLM history
  • Maintainers who unknowingly or willingly accepted LLM-generated contributions from other contributors, if your project otherwise does not involve the heavy use of LLMs

We will also not spend significant amount of time and resources to automatically scan content on Codeberg. So while the following use cases are discouraged (similar to private repositories), they are likely to be tolerated in practice:

  • Side projects and experiments with little resource usage
  • Specific tools and custom scripts that would be unlikely to find a community anyway, even if they were not LLM-generated

However, we also need to be honest about certain use cases that might no longer be welcome on Codeberg. If you see yourself on this list, you don't need to move right away, but there might be other places that better fit your needs:

  • Projects that are created by LLM "agents" in autonomous ways
  • Projects written and maintained with heavy use of LLMs
  • Projects where the amount of resources (e.g. storage, CI/CD) is significantly larger than what the involved amount of people could have created by hand
  • Projects heavily tied to the LLM ecosytem, e.g. LLM-written tools to ease LLM usage
  • Users sending LLM contributions in violation of project's custom policies

You can check the specific change added to the Terms of Use.

sentences.zh: 硬件价格上涨、能源使用和环境破坏——我们都为此付出代价！

blocks.html_en: Strained servers due to nonsensical crawling

sentences.en: Strained servers due to nonsensical crawling
sentences.zh: 由于无意义的爬行而导致服务器紧张

blocks.html_en: In past posts we have already outlined how our infrastructure at Codeberg is regularly put under heavy load from webcrawlers of those companies who plan to ingest all of the code that is hosted on Codeberg for training their LLMs.

sentences.en: In past posts we have already outlined how our infrastructure at Codeberg is regularly put under heavy load from webcrawlers of those companies who plan to ingest all of the code that is hosted on Codeberg for training their LLMs.
sentences.zh: 在过去的文章中，我们已经概述了 Codeberg 的基础设施如何经常承受来自那些计划摄取 Codeberg 上托管的所有代码以培训法学硕士的公司的网络爬虫的重负载。

blocks.html_en: At Codeberg, we are happy to provide free and open access to code. Just run git clone and enjoy.

sentences.en: At Codeberg, we are happy to provide free and open access to code.
sentences.zh: 在 Codeberg，我们很高兴提供免费和开放的代码访问。

sentences.en: Just run git clone and enjoy.
sentences.zh: 只需运行 git clone 即可享受。

blocks.html_en: Unfortunately, these crawlers instead try to read every single page from Codeberg, no matter if it makes sense. This includes all the different issue filter variants, Git history, as well as the actual files at any point in Git history - even if they are still equal.

sentences.en: Unfortunately, these crawlers instead try to read every single page from Codeberg, no matter if it makes sense.
sentences.zh: 不幸的是，这些爬虫反而尝试读取 Codeberg 中的每一页，无论它是否有意义。

sentences.en: This includes all the different issue filter variants, Git history, as well as the actual files at any point in Git history - even if they are still equal.
sentences.zh: 这包括所有不同的问题过滤器变体、Git 历史记录以及 Git 历史记录中任何点的实际文件 - 即使它们仍然相同。

blocks.html_en: These needless accesses create expensive database queries that diminish the service quality for all of us, requires substantial amounts of work from our system administrators, and force us to spend time building defensive mechanisms instead of cool new stuff. Mechanisms that also affect new and existing legitimate users, as we're having to impose limits or outright blocks on their desired workflow; leaving them a worse experience with Codeberg.

sentences.en: These needless accesses create expensive database queries that diminish the service quality for all of us, requires substantial amounts of work from our system administrators, and force us to spend time building defensive mechanisms instead of cool new stuff.
sentences.zh: 这些不必要的访问会产生昂贵的数据库查询，从而降低了我们所有人的服务质量，需要系统管理员进行大量工作，并迫使我们花时间构建防御机制而不是很酷的新东西。

sentences.en: Mechanisms that also affect new and existing legitimate users, as we're having to impose limits or outright blocks on their desired workflow; leaving them a worse experience with Codeberg.
sentences.zh: 也会影响新的和现有的合法用户的机制，因为我们必须对他们所需的工作流程施加限制或彻底阻止；给他们留下了更糟糕的 Codeberg 体验。

blocks.html_en: The development team of none

sentences.en: The development team of none
sentences.zh: 开发团队无一

blocks.html_en: Using LLMs to work with your code gives you a kick of adrenaline. You can develop at a rapid pace, build things as if you had a large team. Only that you have none. In fact, you are (often) alone, working with a statistical machine that turns energy into code.

sentences.en: Using LLMs to work with your code gives you a kick of adrenaline.
sentences.zh: 使用法学硕士来处理您的代码会给您带来肾上腺素飙升的感觉。

sentences.en: You can develop at a rapid pace, build things as if you had a large team.
sentences.zh: 您可以快速开发，像拥有一个大型团队一样构建事物。

sentences.en: Only that you have none.
sentences.zh: 只是你没有。

sentences.en: In fact, you are (often) alone, working with a statistical machine that turns energy into code.
sentences.zh: 事实上，你（经常）独自一人，使用一台将能量转化为代码的统计机器。

blocks.html_en: It seems like many ‘vibe coders’ don't realize that they don't actually have a community around them. They build projects as if they had, and spend resources accordingly. We see projects having a lot of code activity, heavy CI/CD testing, frequent and large release binaries. Sometimes, it feels like the amount of supported platforms exceeds the amount of actual users.

sentences.en: It seems like many ‘vibe coders’ don't realize that they don't actually have a community around them.
sentences.zh: 似乎许多“vibe 程序员”没有意识到他们周围实际上没有社区。

sentences.en: They build projects as if they had, and spend resources accordingly.
sentences.zh: 他们像以前一样构建项目，并相应地花费资源。

sentences.en: We see projects having a lot of code activity, heavy CI/CD testing, frequent and large release binaries.
sentences.zh: 我们看到项目有大量的代码活动、大量的 CI/CD 测试、频繁且大型的发布二进制文件。

sentences.en: Sometimes, it feels like the amount of supported platforms exceeds the amount of actual users.
sentences.zh: 有时，感觉支持的平台数量超过了实际用户数量。

blocks.html_en: To us, it seems ridiculous to see projects with a single developer and virtually no users consuming as much or even more resources than some of the largest community projects on Codeberg, which operate frugal with CI/CD and storage resources. We do not believe it is reasonable for Codeberg to invest our precious donation money into hosting of large ghost projects.

sentences.en: To us, it seems ridiculous to see projects with a single developer and virtually no users consuming as much or even more resources than some of the largest community projects on Codeberg, which operate frugal with CI/CD and storage resources.
sentences.zh: 对我们来说，看到只有一个开发人员且几乎没有用户的项目比 Codeberg 上一些最大的社区项目消耗更多甚至更多的资源，这似乎很荒谬，这些项目在 CI/CD 和存储资源方面非常节俭。

sentences.en: We do not believe it is reasonable for Codeberg to invest our precious donation money into hosting of large ghost projects.
sentences.zh: 我们认为 Codeberg 将我们宝贵的捐款用于托管大型幽灵项目是不合理的。

blocks.html_en: Hardware sourcing is becoming an headache

sentences.en: Hardware sourcing is becoming an headache
sentences.zh: 硬件采购正成为一个令人头疼的问题

blocks.html_en: The training and deployment of LLMs has drastically raised the cost of buying hardware, in particular for SSDs and memory. To give you an example: The type of drive we sourced for € 700 only some years ago has risen to € 3.700 now - and is often out of stock. As a consequence, hosting code on Codeberg is becoming more expensive.

sentences.en: The training and deployment of LLMs has drastically raised the cost of buying hardware, in particular for SSDs and memory.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士的培训和部署极大地提高了购买硬件的成本，特别是 SSD 和内存。

sentences.en: To give you an example: The type of drive we sourced for € 700 only some years ago has risen to € 3.700 now - and is often out of stock.
sentences.zh: 举个例子：几年前我们以 700 欧元采购的驱动器类型现在已涨至 3,700 欧元 - 并且经常缺货。

sentences.en: As a consequence, hosting code on Codeberg is becoming more expensive.
sentences.zh: 因此，在 Codeberg 上托管代码变得更加昂贵。

blocks.html_en: While we are owning our hardware, and are thus not directly impacted by inflating 'cloud' rental costs, it means that replacing or expanding our hardware is now substantially more costly than it used (and needs) to be. While we might be able to afford paying those inflated hardware prices, it is money that we can not spend elsewhere to improve our service and foster the mission of Codeberg.

sentences.en: While we are owning our hardware, and are thus not directly impacted by inflating 'cloud' rental costs, it means that replacing or expanding our hardware is now substantially more costly than it used (and needs) to be.
sentences.zh: 虽然我们拥有自己的硬件，因此不会直接受到“云”租赁成本上涨的影响，但这意味着更换或扩展我们的硬件现在比过去（和需要）的成本要高得多。

sentences.en: While we might be able to afford paying those inflated hardware prices, it is money that we can not spend elsewhere to improve our service and foster the mission of Codeberg.
sentences.zh: 虽然我们可能有能力支付这些虚高的硬件价格，但我们无法将这笔钱花在其他地方来改善我们的服务并促进 Codeberg 的使命。

blocks.html_en: A growing digital divide

sentences.en: A growing digital divide
sentences.zh: 日益扩大的数字鸿沟

blocks.html_en: These price hikes also lead to a growing digital divide: Small and even large operators are endangered by rising costs, while only the largest cloud companies have reliable agreements for hardware. Increasing costs for services like website hosting, storage or compute can be challenging to a lot of small NGOs, local coops, research projects and other usage of digital tools that we considered for granted until recently.

sentences.en: These price hikes also lead to a growing digital divide: Small and even large operators are endangered by rising costs, while only the largest cloud companies have reliable agreements for hardware.
sentences.zh: 这些价格上涨还导致数字鸿沟日益扩大：小型甚至大型运营商都面临成本上涨的威胁，而只有最大的云公司拥有可靠的硬件协议。

sentences.en: Increasing costs for services like website hosting, storage or compute can be challenging to a lot of small NGOs, local coops, research projects and other usage of digital tools that we considered for granted until recently.
sentences.zh: 网站托管、存储或计算等服务成本的增加可能对许多小型非政府组织、当地合作社、研究项目和我们直到最近才认为理所当然的数字工具的其他使用构成挑战。

blocks.html_en: Not only does it become more expensive to run digital infrastructure, even more basic digital tools like computers and smartphones are affected by the rising costs, turning personal computing back into a luxury — but now in a world where digital tools are a de-facto requirement to participate in society. Devices with little compute and storage capacity take away sovereignity from users and move them into the cost trap of cloud providers selling those back to you.

sentences.en: Not only does it become more expensive to run digital infrastructure, even more basic digital tools like computers and smartphones are affected by the rising costs, turning personal computing back into a luxury — but now in a world where digital tools are a de-facto requirement to participate in society.
sentences.zh: 不仅运行数字基础设施变得更加昂贵，甚至计算机和智能手机等更基本的数字工具也受到成本上涨的影响，使个人计算重新成为奢侈品——但现在数字工具已成为参与社会的事实上的要求。

sentences.en: Devices with little compute and storage capacity take away sovereignity from users and move them into the cost trap of cloud providers selling those back to you.
sentences.zh: 计算和存储容量很小的设备剥夺了用户的主权，并将他们带入云提供商将其卖回给您的成本陷阱。

blocks.html_en: Civic infrastructure, its users, and the environment suffer

sentences.en: Civic infrastructure, its users, and the environment suffer
sentences.zh: 市政基础设施、其用户和环境受到影响

blocks.html_en: The negative impacts on infrastructures don't stop there, but also concern more basic civic infrastructure: Due to the energy and water demands that are inherent to the data centers built specifically for training LLMs, many communities already today experience rising consumer costs for both electricity and drinking water. And beyond the rising costs, those living close to the data centers are directly affected by increasing air and noise pollution.

sentences.en: The negative impacts on infrastructures don't stop there, but also concern more basic civic infrastructure: Due to the energy and water demands that are inherent to the data centers built specifically for training LLMs, many communities already today experience rising consumer costs for both electricity and drinking water.
sentences.zh: 对基础设施的负面影响还不止于此，还涉及更基本的市政基础设施：由于专门为培训法学硕士而建造的数据中心固有的能源和水需求，许多社区如今已经经历了电力和饮用水消费者成本的上升。

sentences.en: And beyond the rising costs, those living close to the data centers are directly affected by increasing air and noise pollution.
sentences.zh: 除了成本上升之外，居住在数据中心附近的人们还直接受到空气和噪音污染加剧的影响。

blocks.html_en: To be able to power up those data centers, the companies behind them are also actively lobbying to be exempt from environmental regulations: In Frankfurt, data centers already now consume 40% of the local electricity, and demand is rising. To meet this demand, they want to use fossil fuels.

sentences.en: To be able to power up those data centers, the companies behind them are also actively lobbying to be exempt from environmental regulations: In Frankfurt, data centers already now consume 40% of the local electricity, and demand is rising.
sentences.zh: 为了能够为这些数据中心供电，其背后的公司也在积极游说，要求豁免环境法规：在法兰克福，数据中心现在已经消耗了当地 40% 的电力，而且需求还在不断增长。

sentences.en: To meet this demand, they want to use fossil fuels.
sentences.zh: 为了满足这种需求，他们希望使用化石燃料。

blocks.html_en: Collaboration at danger

sentences.en: Collaboration at danger
sentences.zh: 危险中的合作

blocks.html_en: It is not purely the digital and civic infrastructures that are impacted by the use of LLMs. The free/libre software ecosystem, of which we consider Codeberg an important part of, is a social phenomenon centered on collaboration. Working in this way is only possible thanks to free sharing and mutual learning. This includes even very small tools that are shared and re-used and around which collaboration can start out. In contrast, by adopting LLMs people tend to code single-use software from scratch. While this leads to an increase in 'shared' code, it is mostly code that not only has not been 'written' by anyone but is also not maintained by anyone.

sentences.en: It is not purely the digital and civic infrastructures that are impacted by the use of LLMs.
sentences.zh: 受法学硕士的使用影响的不仅仅是数字和公民基础设施。

sentences.en: The free/libre software ecosystem, of which we consider Codeberg an important part of, is a social phenomenon centered on collaboration.
sentences.zh: 我们认为 Codeberg 是自由软件生态系统的重要组成部分，它是一种以协作为中心的社会现象。

sentences.en: Working in this way is only possible thanks to free sharing and mutual learning.
sentences.zh: 只有通过自由分享和相互学习，这种工作方式才有可能实现。

sentences.en: This includes even very small tools that are shared and re-used and around which collaboration can start out.
sentences.zh: 这甚至包括共享和重复使用的非常小的工具，并且可以围绕这些工具开始协作。

sentences.en: In contrast, by adopting LLMs people tend to code single-use software from scratch.
sentences.zh: 相比之下，通过采用法学硕士，人们倾向于从头开始编写一次性软件的代码。

sentences.en: While this leads to an increase in 'shared' code, it is mostly code that not only has not been 'written' by anyone but is also not maintained by anyone.
sentences.zh: 虽然这导致“共享”代码的增加，但大部分代码不仅没有由任何人“编写”，而且也没有由任何人维护。

blocks.html_en: Losing trust in each other

sentences.en: Losing trust in each other
sentences.zh: 彼此失去信任

blocks.html_en: The widespread use of LLMs in FLOSS is instead becoming a multidimensional attack on the trust between contributors and the very idea of convivial collaboration itself. Maintainers are under an increased work-load due to people submitting (often well-meaning) low-effort, LLM-generated contributions that require substantial amounts of time to review. At the same time it is becoming increasingly less clear which projects are maintained by experienced developers and which ones are LLM-generated without any meaningful human oversight and input. In the case of copyleft projects, LLMs additionally also lead to 'license laundering', where copyleft code is stripped of its reciprocity requirements by 'generating' it out of the training data.

sentences.en: The widespread use of LLMs in FLOSS is instead becoming a multidimensional attack on the trust between contributors and the very idea of convivial collaboration itself.
sentences.zh: 相反，法学硕士在 FLOSS 中的广泛使用正在成为对贡献者之间的信任以及愉快协作本身理念的多维攻击。

sentences.en: Maintainers are under an increased work-load due to people submitting (often well-meaning) low-effort, LLM-generated contributions that require substantial amounts of time to review.
sentences.zh: 由于人们提交（通常是善意的）低费力的法学硕士生成的贡献，需要大量的时间来审查，维护者的工作量增加了。

sentences.en: At the same time it is becoming increasingly less clear which projects are maintained by experienced developers and which ones are LLM-generated without any meaningful human oversight and input.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，哪些项目是由经验丰富的开发人员维护的，哪些项目是法学硕士在没有任何有意义的人工监督和输入的情况下生成的，这一点变得越来越不清楚。

sentences.en: In the case of copyleft projects, LLMs additionally also lead to 'license laundering', where copyleft code is stripped of its reciprocity requirements by 'generating' it out of the training data.
sentences.zh: 就 Copyleft 项目而言，法学硕士还会导致“许可洗钱”，即通过从训练数据中“生成”Copyleft 代码，从而剥夺其互惠要求。

blocks.html_en: We observe an increasing trend of mistrusting each other, up to the point where people who put in actual effort to analyze issues or share their suggestions are being accused of having used LLMs when they did not. At the same time, others instruct their LLMs to hide their traces and actively avoid common patterns, prompting others in reviewing contributions and communication more carefully for signs of machine generation.

sentences.en: We observe an increasing trend of mistrusting each other, up to the point where people who put in actual effort to analyze issues or share their suggestions are being accused of having used LLMs when they did not.
sentences.zh: 我们观察到相互不信任的趋势日益明显，以至于那些付出实际努力来分析问题或分享建议的人被指责为使用了法学硕士，而实际上他们并没有这样做。

sentences.en: At the same time, others instruct their LLMs to hide their traces and actively avoid common patterns, prompting others in reviewing contributions and communication more carefully for signs of machine generation.
sentences.zh: 与此同时，其他人指示他们的法学硕士隐藏自己的踪迹并积极避免常见模式，促使其他人更仔细地审查贡献和沟通，以寻找机器生成的迹象。

blocks.html_en: Entering a vicious cycle

sentences.en: Entering a vicious cycle
sentences.zh: 进入恶性循环

blocks.html_en: Together, these forces make collaboration not only harder but also less rewarding: With the transaction cost of collaboration increasing, people are becoming less likely to contribute to creating high-quality software projects and more likely to 'vibecode' a one-off software that is specific to your need, and won't evolve beyond. We get a vicious cycle where collaboration is becoming less and less rewarding, while the amount of single-use software that's unmaintained and never sees any improvements is going up.

sentences.en: Together, these forces make collaboration not only harder but also less rewarding: With the transaction cost of collaboration increasing, people are becoming less likely to contribute to creating high-quality software projects and more likely to 'vibecode' a one-off software that is specific to your need, and won't evolve beyond.
sentences.zh: 这些力量加在一起，不仅使协作变得更加困难，而且回报也随之减少：随着协作交易成本的增加，人们越来越不可能为创建高质量的软件项目做出贡献，而更有可能“编码”一种专门针对您的需求且不会超越您的需求的一次性软件。

sentences.en: We get a vicious cycle where collaboration is becoming less and less rewarding, while the amount of single-use software that's unmaintained and never sees any improvements is going up.
sentences.zh: 我们陷入了一个恶性循环，协作的回报越来越少，而未经维护且从未看到任何改进的一次性软件的数量却在增加。

blocks.html_en: Although often well intentioned, sharing the result of an prompt and calling it "libre software" does not make the world a better place. Codeberg is not and does not want to be a place to dump such generated single-use software that no one else will ever look at. We are a place for people to collaborate and improve software together. Within this context, the recent votes can be understood as a reconfirmation of those principles: As we want to center on human collaboration, we will not actively support or engage in the creation of LLMs and will not put our limited resources to use for storing single-use software that would pollute our FLOSS commons.

sentences.en: Although often well intentioned, sharing the result of an prompt and calling it "libre software" does not make the world a better place.
sentences.zh: 尽管通常是出于善意，但共享提示结果并称其为“自由软件”并不会让世界变得更美好。

sentences.en: Codeberg is not and does not want to be a place to dump such generated single-use software that no one else will ever look at.
sentences.zh: Codeberg 不是也不想成为一个转储此类生成的一次性软件而没有人会看到的地方。

sentences.en: We are a place for people to collaborate and improve software together.
sentences.zh: 我们是人们共同协作和改进软件的地方。

sentences.en: Within this context, the recent votes can be understood as a reconfirmation of those principles: As we want to center on human collaboration, we will not actively support or engage in the creation of LLMs and will not put our limited resources to use for storing single-use software that would pollute our FLOSS commons.
sentences.zh: 在这种背景下，最近的投票可以被理解为对这些原则的再次确认：由于我们希望以人类协作为中心，因此我们不会积极支持或参与法学硕士的创建，也不会将我们有限的资源用于存储会污染我们的 FLOSS 公共资源的一次性软件。

blocks.html_en: Evolving our terms

sentences.en: Evolving our terms
sentences.zh: 不断发展我们的条款

blocks.html_en: Changing our Terms of Use sends a strong signal about our mission and projects we want to support. Having said that, you won't see a mass-deletion of content within days. Our moderation team will not start off generating an exhaustive list of affected repositories to remove. Instead, using cases like the examples below we will start operationalizing the new rules. We're humans at the other end, who care deeply about free/libre software projects and communities.

sentences.en: Changing our Terms of Use sends a strong signal about our mission and projects we want to support.
sentences.zh: 更改我们的使用条款发出了关于我们的使命和我们想要支持的项目的强烈信号。

sentences.en: Having said that, you won't see a mass-deletion of content within days.
sentences.zh: 话虽如此，您不会在几天内看到内容被大量删除。

sentences.en: Our moderation team will not start off generating an exhaustive list of affected repositories to remove.
sentences.zh: 我们的审核团队不会开始生成要删除的受影响存储库的详尽列表。

sentences.en: Instead, using cases like the examples below we will start operationalizing the new rules.
sentences.zh: 相反，我们将使用如下示例之类的案例开始实施新规则。

sentences.en: We're humans at the other end, who care deeply about free/libre software projects and communities.
sentences.zh: 我们是另一端的人类，我们非常关心自由/自由软件项目和社区。

blocks.html_en: We acknowledge that many developers have started to embrace LLMs as a tool in their workflows. Some use it extensively and rarely code by hand, others delegate only specific tasks to it. We understand that you want to know how the change affects your projects going forward. While we can't give an easy answer, we'll share some remarks that should address most of the concerns raised in the discussion.

sentences.en: We acknowledge that many developers have started to embrace LLMs as a tool in their workflows.
sentences.zh: 我们承认许多开发人员已经开始将法学硕士作为其工作流程中的工具。

sentences.en: Some use it extensively and rarely code by hand, others delegate only specific tasks to it.
sentences.zh: 有些人广泛使用它，很少手工编写代码，其他人只将特定任务委托给它。

sentences.en: We understand that you want to know how the change affects your projects going forward.
sentences.zh: 我们理解您想知道变更如何影响您的项目的未来。

sentences.en: While we can't give an easy answer, we'll share some remarks that should address most of the concerns raised in the discussion.
sentences.zh: 虽然我们无法给出简单的答案，但我们将分享一些评论，这些评论应该可以解决讨论中提出的大多数问题。

blocks.html_en: Some early, but informal guidelines

sentences.en: Some early, but informal guidelines
sentences.zh: 一些早期但非正式的指导方针

blocks.html_en: If your work fits into these cases, it is unlikely that you are affected at all:

sentences.en: If your work fits into these cases, it is unlikely that you are affected at all:
sentences.zh: 如果您的工作属于这些情况，那么您根本不可能受到影响：

blocks.html_en: • Projects who have an active community that cares about and maintains the software

content_html_zh:
简而言之：

  • Codeberg e 就两项关于“人工智能”和大型语言模型 (LLM) 的动议进行了投票。 五、 会员并通过。
  • 我们承诺不会使用您的任何数据来培训法学硕士，并解释计划的使用条款变更对“vibe-coded”项目意味着什么。
  • 我们认为法学硕士危及整个自由/自由软件生态系统。

科德博格 e. 五、 年度大会是将权力交到我们活跃成员手中的会议。 提案会进行现场讨论，然后进行异步投票。

由于大型语言模型 (LLM) 是一项新兴但有争议的技术，因此其中两票与 Codeberg 对这项技术的立场有关也就不足为奇了。 为期 14 天的投票期于昨天结束，两项提案均被接受。

第一个投票是关于 Codeberg e 的声明。 V. 关于使用您的数据培训法学硕士的立场。

正如我们的隐私政策中所述，“我们不想需要您的数据”，这也适用于使用我们的用户和项目数据来使用或训练生成“AI”：Codeberg forge 及其相关服务不会也不会使用项目和用户的代码或数据来训练“人工智能”工具，例如大型语言模型，其目的是根据训练输入创建建模输出。 作为一个协会，我们认为这些技术与负责任地创建和维护自由开源软件不兼容。

第二次投票更具争议性，但也以 358 票同意、144 票反对（14 票弃权）获得通过，投票率很高，活跃成员约为 50%。 这意味着我们的使用条款将发生变化，以禁止“振动编码项目”。 我们将在文章末尾分享有关实际影响的想法。

我们都为饥饿的法学硕士付出了代价

法学硕士是一项成本非常昂贵的技术，而且随着提供法学硕士的公司必须开始收回投资，这些成本不断上升。 它们不仅对于那些使用并明确订阅这些服务的人来说成本高昂。 这些成本不仅隐藏在“正常”云和服务订阅中，这些订阅还为您从未要求过的“创新新功能”提供交叉融资。 法学硕士的成本如此之高，以至于公司将成本大规模地外部化——对于那些不使用它们的人以及整个社会来说。 硬件价格上涨、能源使用和环境破坏——我们都为此付出代价！

由于无意义的爬行而导致服务器紧张

在过去的文章中，我们已经概述了 Codeberg 的基础设施如何经常承受来自那些计划摄取 Codeberg 上托管的所有代码以培训法学硕士的公司的网络爬虫的重负载。

在 Codeberg，我们很高兴提供免费和开放的代码访问。 只需运行 git clone 即可享受。

不幸的是，这些爬虫反而尝试读取 Codeberg 中的每一页，无论它是否有意义。 这包括所有不同的问题过滤器变体、Git 历史记录以及 Git 历史记录中任何点的实际文件 - 即使它们仍然相同。

这些不必要的访问会产生昂贵的数据库查询，从而降低了我们所有人的服务质量，需要系统管理员进行大量工作，并迫使我们花时间构建防御机制而不是很酷的新东西。 也会影响新的和现有的合法用户的机制，因为我们必须对他们所需的工作流程施加限制或彻底阻止；给他们留下了更糟糕的 Codeberg 体验。

开发团队无一

使用法学硕士来处理您的代码会给您带来肾上腺素飙升的感觉。 您可以快速开发，像拥有一个大型团队一样构建事物。 只是你没有。 事实上，你（经常）独自一人，使用一台将能量转化为代码的统计机器。

似乎许多“vibe 程序员”没有意识到他们周围实际上没有社区。 他们像以前一样构建项目，并相应地花费资源。 我们看到项目有大量的代码活动、大量的 CI/CD 测试、频繁且大型的发布二进制文件。 有时，感觉支持的平台数量超过了实际用户数量。

对我们来说，看到只有一个开发人员且几乎没有用户的项目比 Codeberg 上一些最大的社区项目消耗更多甚至更多的资源，这似乎很荒谬，这些项目在 CI/CD 和存储资源方面非常节俭。 我们认为 Codeberg 将我们宝贵的捐款用于托管大型幽灵项目是不合理的。

硬件采购正成为一个令人头疼的问题

法学硕士的培训和部署极大地提高了购买硬件的成本，特别是 SSD 和内存。 举个例子：几年前我们以 700 欧元采购的驱动器类型现在已涨至 3,700 欧元 - 并且经常缺货。 因此，在 Codeberg 上托管代码变得更加昂贵。

虽然我们拥有自己的硬件，因此不会直接受到“云”租赁成本上涨的影响，但这意味着更换或扩展我们的硬件现在比过去（和需要）的成本要高得多。 虽然我们可能有能力支付这些虚高的硬件价格，但我们无法将这笔钱花在其他地方来改善我们的服务并促进 Codeberg 的使命。

日益扩大的数字鸿沟

这些价格上涨还导致数字鸿沟日益扩大：小型甚至大型运营商都面临成本上涨的威胁，而只有最大的云公司拥有可靠的硬件协议。 网站托管、存储或计算等服务成本的增加可能对许多小型非政府组织、当地合作社、研究项目和我们直到最近才认为理所当然的数字工具的其他使用构成挑战。

不仅运行数字基础设施变得更加昂贵，甚至计算机和智能手机等更基本的数字工具也受到成本上涨的影响，使个人计算重新成为奢侈品——但现在数字工具已成为参与社会的事实上的要求。 计算和存储容量很小的设备剥夺了用户的主权，并将他们带入云提供商将其卖回给您的成本陷阱。

市政基础设施、其用户和环境受到影响

对基础设施的负面影响还不止于此，还涉及更基本的市政基础设施：由于专门为培训法学硕士而建造的数据中心固有的能源和水需求，许多社区如今已经经历了电力和饮用水消费者成本的上升。 除了成本上升之外，居住在数据中心附近的人们还直接受到空气和噪音污染加剧的影响。

为了能够为这些数据中心供电，其背后的公司也在积极游说，要求豁免环境法规：在法兰克福，数据中心现在已经消耗了当地 40% 的电力，而且需求还在不断增长。 为了满足这种需求，他们希望使用化石燃料。

危险中的合作

受法学硕士的使用影响的不仅仅是数字和公民基础设施。 我们认为 Codeberg 是自由软件生态系统的重要组成部分，它是一种以协作为中心的社会现象。 只有通过自由分享和相互学习，这种工作方式才有可能实现。 这甚至包括共享和重复使用的非常小的工具，并且可以围绕这些工具开始协作。 相比之下，通过采用法学硕士，人们倾向于从头开始编写一次性软件的代码。 虽然这导致“共享”代码的增加，但大部分代码不仅没有由任何人“编写”，而且也没有由任何人维护。

彼此失去信任

相反，法学硕士在 FLOSS 中的广泛使用正在成为对贡献者之间的信任以及愉快协作本身理念的多维攻击。 由于人们提交（通常是善意的）低费力的法学硕士生成的贡献，需要大量的时间来审查，维护者的工作量增加了。 与此同时，哪些项目是由经验丰富的开发人员维护的，哪些项目是法学硕士在没有任何有意义的人工监督和输入的情况下生成的，这一点变得越来越不清楚。 就 Copyleft 项目而言，法学硕士还会导致“许可洗钱”，即通过从训练数据中“生成”Copyleft 代码，从而剥夺其互惠要求。

我们观察到相互不信任的趋势日益明显，以至于那些付出实际努力来分析问题或分享建议的人被指责为使用了法学硕士，而实际上他们并没有这样做。 与此同时，其他人指示他们的法学硕士隐藏自己的踪迹并积极避免常见模式，促使其他人更仔细地审查贡献和沟通，以寻找机器生成的迹象。

进入恶性循环

这些力量加在一起，不仅使协作变得更加困难，而且回报也随之减少：随着协作交易成本的增加，人们越来越不可能为创建高质量的软件项目做出贡献，而更有可能“编码”一种专门针对您的需求且不会超越您的需求的一次性软件。 我们陷入了一个恶性循环，协作的回报越来越少，而未经维护且从未看到任何改进的一次性软件的数量却在增加。

尽管通常是出于善意，但共享提示结果并称其为“自由软件”并不会让世界变得更美好。 Codeberg 不是也不想成为一个转储此类生成的一次性软件而没有人会看到的地方。 我们是人们共同协作和改进软件的地方。 在这种背景下，最近的投票可以被理解为对这些原则的再次确认：由于我们希望以人类协作为中心，因此我们不会积极支持或参与法学硕士的创建，也不会将我们有限的资源用于存储会污染我们的 FLOSS 公共资源的一次性软件。

不断发展我们的条款

更改我们的使用条款发出了关于我们的使命和我们想要支持的项目的强烈信号。 话虽如此，您不会在几天内看到内容被大量删除。 我们的审核团队不会开始生成要删除的受影响存储库的详尽列表。 相反，我们将使用如下示例之类的案例开始实施新规则。 我们是另一端的人类，我们非常关心自由/自由软件项目和社区。

我们承认许多开发人员已经开始将法学硕士作为其工作流程中的工具。 有些人广泛使用它，很少手工编写代码，其他人只将特定任务委托给它。 我们理解您想知道变更如何影响您的项目的未来。 虽然我们无法给出简单的答案，但我们将分享一些评论，这些评论应该可以解决讨论中提出的大多数问题。

一些早期但非正式的指导方针

如果您的工作属于这些情况，那么您根本不可能受到影响：

  • 拥有关心和维护软件的活跃社区的项目
  • 具有重要LLM预科历史的项目
  • 如果您的项目不涉及大量使用 LLM，则维护者在不知情或自愿的情况下接受了其他贡献者由 LLM 生成的贡献

我们也不会花费大量时间和资源来自动扫描 Codeberg 上的内容。 因此，虽然不鼓励使用以下用例（类似于私有存储库），但在实践中它们可能会被容忍：

  • 资源占用很少的副项目和实验
  • 无论如何，即使它们不是 LLM 生成的，也不太可能找到社区的特定工具和自定义脚本

然而，我们还需要诚实地对待 Codeberg 可能不再受欢迎的某些用例。 如果您看到自己在此列表中，则无需立即搬家，但可能还有其他更适合您需求的地方：

  • 由法学硕士“代理人”以自主方式创建的项目
  • 大量使用法学硕士编写和维护的项目
  • 资源量（例如存储、CI/CD）明显大于所涉及人员手动创建的资源量的项目
  • 与 LLM 生态系统密切相关的项目，例如LLM 编写的工具可简化 LLM 使用
  • 用户违反项目的自定义政策发送 LLM 贡献

您可以查看使用条款中添加的具体更改。
